key_insight: The paper models AI delegation as a Bayesian sequential decision problem, using belief updates to adapt how much authority to give AI as uncertainty changes over time.
engineering_relevance: This provides a quantitative governance framework for high-stakes engineering and operational settings where AI recommendations must be trusted, limited, or escalated dynamically.
method: Formulate governance as a Partially Observable Markov Decision Process (POMDP).
Use Bayesian inference to estimate the informational state of AI outputs and evidence quality.
Optimize delegated authority sequentially under governance objectives and uncertainty.
evidence_signal: The policy is evaluated with synthetic stress tests, confidence-perturbation robustness checks, forecast validation, governance-appetite sensitivity, fragile-AI early-warning experiments, and benchmarking against five baseline governance strategies.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the Bayesian model and assumed governance objectives, so mis-specified beliefs or reward settings could lead to overly conservative or overly permissive delegation.
paper_title: Adaptive AI Delegation under Uncertainty: A Bayesian Governance Policy for Sequential Decision Authority
